<region>
  <regionName>Starfall Station</regionName>
  <regionDescription>Starfall Station hangs in eternal orbit around the amber-and-crimson gas giant Prometheus, its ring-shaped hull glittering with the running lights of a thousand docked vessels and the soft blue glow of atmospheric containment fields. Once a modest refueling outpost, the station has grown into a thriving crossroads where Terran merchants haggle with elven nobles over crystalline data-slates, android philosophers debate consciousness in zero-g cafés, and Free Cluster smugglers move contraband through maintenance shafts older than the Federation itself. The station's architecture reflects its hybrid heritage: sleek Federation alloy corridors twist unexpectedly into vaulted chambers where elven warding sigils pulse alongside Terran power conduits, and the ever-present hum of machinery carries undertones of something almost like music.</regionDescription>
  <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
  <relativeLevel>1</relativeLevel>
  <numImportantNPCs>3</numImportantNPCs>
  <weather>
    <hasDynamicWeather>false</hasDynamicWeather>
  </weather>
  <locations>

    <location>
      <name>Docking Bay 17</name>
      <description>The cavernous bay stretches before you like the throat of some metallic beast, its walls scarred by decades of clumsy piloting and hurried repairs. Magnetic clamps hold a dozen vessels of varying sizes in skeletal embrace—sleek Federation couriers with mirror-polished hulls, battered Free Cluster freighters patched with mismatched plating, and one magnificent elven courier whose organic curves seem grown rather than built, its surface etched with slow-moving constellations of light. The air tastes of ozone and hot metal, punctuated by the shriek of plasma torches and the barked commands of deck crews in three languages.

A small shuttle for hire sits on pad seven, its pilot dozing in the cockpit with her feet on the dash, while the massive elven starship *Luminara's Sigh* dominates the VIP berths—its airlock sealed, its crew invisible behind mirrored viewports. Overhead, the station's ring curves away into haze, giving the unsettling impression that you stand inside a world rather than upon one.</description>
      <shortDescription>A sprawling docking bay where vessels of every allegiance rest between voyages, thick with the industry of departure and arrival.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Connects to Starfall Commons via the main pedestrian causeway, and to the Promenade through a service corridor used by crew with shore leave.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>4</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Starfall Commons</exit>
        <exit>The Promenade</exit>
      </exits>
      <vehicles>
        <vehicle>
          <name>Small Shuttle for Hire</name>
          <description>A boxy Federation surplus craft, its white paint faded to cream, with a cockpit barely large enough for pilot and two passengers. The rear compartment contains jump seats and a small cargo net. Ion thrusters glow faintly blue at rest.</description>
          <shortDescription>A battered but reliable short-range shuttle.</shortDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <size>large</size>
          <numNPCs>1</numNPCs>
          <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
        </vehicle>
        <vehicle>
          <name>Luminara's Sigh</name>
          <description>An elven void-runner of impossible grace, its hull grown from crystallized starlight and memory-metal, currently sealed and silent. The ship seems to breathe, its surface patterns shifting like slow aurora. No Terran dockworker will approach within ten meters.</description>
          <shortDescription>A magnificent elven starship of living crystal and light.</shortDescription>
          <controllingFaction>House Valdris</controllingFaction>
          <size>huge</size>
          <numNPCs>0</numNPCs>
          <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
        </vehicle>
      </vehicles>
      <regionExits>
        <stubRegion type="connectedRegion">
          <regionName>Terran Federation Space</regionName>
          <regionDescription>The organized sectors of humanity's greatest political achievement, where Core Worlds glitter with wealth and military patrols maintain order through overwhelming presence. Here corporate towers rise above arcologies, and the Federation Navy's chrome hulls reflect the light of a thousand stable stars. Yet even in this bastion of human power, elven enclaves and android communities carve their own spaces, creating a patchwork of cultures united more by trade than ideology.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>Terran Federation</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>2</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Adjacent</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
        <stubRegion type="connectedRegion">
          <regionName>Celestial Dynasty Border Territories</regionName>
          <regionDescription>The contested edges of elven space, where ancient noble houses maintain their influence through a combination of overwhelming magical tradition and surprisingly modern military adaptation. Fleetships the size of moons drift between worlds that have known elven rule for millennia, their surfaces grown rather than built, their interiors labyrinthine with history. Federation corporations and Free Cluster opportunists both test the Dynasty's patience here, finding profit in the gaps between immortal patience and mortal ambition.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>Celestial Dynasty</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>2</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Adjacent</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
        <stubRegion type="connectedRegion">
          <regionName>Free Cluster Shipping Lanes</regionName>
          <regionDescription>A tangled web of semi-legal trade routes winding through systems no major power claims, where Eclipse Fleet privateers and independent captains alike find fortune and peril in equal measure. These lanes connect hidden stations, temporary refueling points, and the kind of places where questions are dangerous and credits spend regardless of their origin. The Federation patrols them sporadically, the Dynasty ignores them disdainfully, and those who travel them learn quickly that loyalty is a luxury and survival is the only currency that matters.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>Eclipse Privateering Fleet</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>3</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Adjacent</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
        <stubRegion type="connectedRegion">
          <regionName>Deep Space</regionName>
          <regionDescription>The vast darkness between the mapped regions, where navigation beacons grow sparse and the void itself seems to watch with ancient, hungry attention. Here prospectors seek unclaimed resources, cultists perform rituals far from prying eyes, and something—perhaps the Void Court itself—presses against the boundaries of reality. Ships that venture too deep sometimes return with empty crew compartments and log entries that make no chronological sense.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>4</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Adjacent</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
      </regionExits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>The Nebula Cantina</name>
      <description>Holographic nebulae swirl across the ceiling in slow, hypnotic patterns—reds and purples and impossible greens that shift with the mood of the crowd below. The Cantina occupies three converted cargo containers welded together in some forgotten century, their original markings still visible beneath layers of graffiti, diplomatic seals, and protective wards. Round tables of polished meteorite iron cluster around a central bar where an android mixologist with chrome arms and a vintage oil-stained apron serves drinks whose recipes span three species and two thousand years.

The lighting is deliberately dim, encouraging the kind of conversations that flourish in shadow: mercenaries negotiating contracts in whispered Federation Standard, elven exiles speaking in the musical cadences of High Court dialect, smugglers flashing encrypted data-chips across tables where no recording device functions. A small stage hosts musicians when the mood strikes—sometimes a human with a neural synthesizer, sometimes an elven bard whose voice carries harmonics that make androids weep without understanding why. The air smells of ozone, fermented star-anise, and the particular ionic charge of secrets about to be shared.</description>
      <shortDescription>A dimly lit watering hole where the station's many factions mingle uneasily over drinks that cross cultural boundaries.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Opens directly onto Starfall Commons, with a discreet back exit leading to Cargo Hold 7 for those who prefer unseen departures.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>4</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>1</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Starfall Commons</exit>
        <exit>Cargo Hold 7</exit>
      </exits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Starfall Commons</name>
      <description>The heart of the station beats here in the Commons, a vaulted cylindrical space where three levels of shops and stalls spiral around a central null-g core where children and show-offs alike practice their zero-gravity acrobatics. The architecture is aggressively eclectic: Federation modular construction supports elven-grown crystal awnings, while android artisans have installed kinetic sculptures that respond to crowd emotions with shifting patterns of light and sound. Merchants cry their wares in overlapping cacophony—spice traders from the Dynasty's outer worlds, Terran tech-brokers with cases of military-grade implants, Free Cluster scavengers selling "salvage" whose provenance no one questions.

The crowd moves in patterns that suggest choreography, each species and faction maintaining invisible boundaries even in this theoretically neutral space. A human family gawks at an elven clothier whose fabrics shift color with the wearer's mood; android pilgrims visit a shrine where the first synthetic to achieve legal personhood once gave a speech; off-duty station security watch everything with the relaxed alertness of professionals who know that today's incident will be tomorrow's paperwork. The air carries a hundred scents—exotic, synthetic, familiar, strange—blended into something uniquely Starfall.</description>
      <shortDescription>The station's bustling commercial heart, where three spiraling levels of merchants and shoppers create constant motion.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Central hub connecting to Docking Bay 17, The Nebula Cantina, The Promenade, and the Holo-Arcade; the monorail station provides access to distant ring sections.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>4</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Docking Bay 17</exit>
        <exit>The Nebula Cantina</exit>
        <exit>The Promenade</exit>
        <exit>Holo-Arcade</exit>
      </exits>
      <vehicles>
        <vehicle>
          <name>Starfall Ring Monorail</name>
          <description>A sleek magnetic train that runs the circumference of the station's main ring, its cars offering panoramic views of Prometheus's swirling storms through transparent hull sections. The automated voice announces stops in Federation Standard, High Elven, and Synthetic Binary.</description>
          <shortDescription>Magnetic train circumnavigating the station ring.</shortDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <size>large</size>
          <destinations>
            <destination>
              <location>Station Command</location>
            </destination>
            <destination>
              <location>Med-Bay Alpha</location>
            </destination>
          </destinations>
          <numNPCs>2</numNPCs>
          <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
        </vehicle>
      </vehicles>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Ryne's Berth Exterior</name>
      <description>The residential ring's corridor opens into a modest plaza where artificial sunlight—rare on the station—filters through crystalline panels salvaged from some elven vessel. Planters overflow with engineered greenery that requires neither soil nor water, their leaves shifting from Terran green to Dynasty violet depending on the angle of view. Several doors open onto this space, each marked with the glowing sigil of a different housing collective, but the largest and most weathered bears the name "Ryne's Berth" in hand-painted letters that have been touched up so many times the original font is barely discernible.

The building itself is unremarkable—a blocky Federation prefab structure, three stories, its hull plating pocked with micrometeorite scars that no one has bothered to repair. Yet the windows glow warm in the perpetual station-night, and the small balcony on the second floor hosts a garden of hydroponic tomatoes and elven whisper-flowers that should not thrive together but somehow do. A battered bench sits beneath a maintenance access panel that local kids have turned into an unofficial message board, its surface layered with encrypted contact chips, hand-written notes in six languages, and one persistent piece of graffiti that reads "THE VOID REMEMBERS" in letters that seem to shift when viewed peripherally.</description>
      <shortDescription>A modest residential building in a rare sunlit plaza, its mixed heritage evident in every detail.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Located off a residential corridor from Starfall Commons; connects to the Promenade via a maintenance walkway used by locals.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>1</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Starfall Commons</exit>
        <exit>The Promenade</exit>
      </exits>
      <regionExits>
        <stubRegion type="building">
          <regionName>Ryne's Berth Interior</regionName>
          <regionDescription>A converted cargo hauler section turned residential complex, where travelers and station workers rent cramped but private quarters. The interior corridors are narrow, the walls thick with sound-dampening foam, and the common kitchen always smells of someone else's nostalgia. The current proprietor, a retired android navigator, maintains strict neutrality about guests' business.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>1</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Within</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
      </regionExits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Station Command</name>
      <description>The administrative heart of Starfall Station occupies the forward section of the ring, its curved observation windows offering an unparalleled view of Prometheus's churning atmosphere and the distant glitter of the orbital tether that anchors the station against tidal drift. The architecture here is pure Federation functionalism—gray walls, standardized lighting, ergonomic workstations arranged in patterns that suggest efficiency over inspiration—yet elven influence shows in the subtle warding circles etched into floor panels and the living wood accents that Federation designers would never have chosen but somehow never removed. Officers in station livery move with purpose between holographic displays showing traffic patterns, life support metrics, and the ever-shifting roster of vessels requesting docking clearance.

The command center proper is elevated, accessible by a short flight of stairs that puts the duty officers literally above the administrative drones below. The current Stationmaster, a human cyborg with visible neural interface ports along her temples, holds court here during the day cycle, her decisions shaping the fortunes of everyone who passes through Starfall's jurisdiction. Tension lives in this space—a delicate balance between Federation regulations, Dynasty protocols, and the practical necessities of running a station that no single power truly controls. The air tastes of recycled oxygen and political possibility, and everyone speaks just loudly enough to be overheard by someone who matters.</description>
      <shortDescription>The station's administrative nerve center, where Federation efficiency meets elven tradition under the gaze of a cyborg Stationmaster.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>Terran Federation</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>1</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Accessible via monorail from Starfall Commons; connects directly to Med-Bay Alpha through secure corridors for emergency response.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>3</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Med-Bay Alpha</exit>
      </exits>
      <vehicles>
        <vehicle>
          <name>Starfall Ring Monorail</name>
          <description>A sleek magnetic train that runs the circumference of the station's main ring, its cars offering panoramic views of Prometheus's swirling storms through transparent hull sections. The automated voice announces stops in Federation Standard, High Elven, and Synthetic Binary.</description>
          <shortDescription>Magnetic train circumnavigating the station ring.</shortDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <size>large</size>
          <destinations>
            <destination>
              <location>Starfall Commons</location>
            </destination>
            <destination>
              <location>Med-Bay Alpha</location>
            </destination>
          </destinations>
          <numNPCs>2</numNPCs>
          <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
        </vehicle>
      </vehicles>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>The Promenade</name>
      <description>Where the Commons serves commerce, the Promenade caters to leisure—a sweeping curved deck that follows the station's rotation, its transparent hull sections offering the illusion of standing upon nothing, suspended above Prometheus's infinite storms. The view is the attraction here, and the businesses that line the Promenade have arranged themselves to maximize it: restaurants with tables pressed against the transparency, meditation chambers where elven nobles practice ancient breathing exercises, a single Terran chapel whose pews face outward rather than toward any altar. The lighting shifts with the station's artificial day-cycle, currently golden afternoon that makes the gas giant's cloud bands glow like molten copper.

Couples stroll arm-in-arm, their species mixing in combinations that would have been scandalous three generations ago and are now merely interesting. A street performer—an android with a body of polished brass and a voice trained in Dynasty opera—holds a small crowd spellbound with a ballad about the Great Convergence. Children press their faces to the transparent hull, searching for the station's shadow against the clouds below, while their parents pretend not to worry about micrometeorites and structural integrity. The air is cleaner here, filtered to perfection, carrying only the faintest scent of the gardens that someone maintains in defiance of all practical necessity.</description>
      <shortDescription>A leisure deck with transparent hull sections offering breathtaking views of Prometheus's storms.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Connects to Docking Bay 17 for arriving crew with shore leave, to Starfall Commons for shopping, and to Ryne's Berth via the maintenance walkway.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>3</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Docking Bay 17</exit>
        <exit>Starfall Commons</exit>
        <exit>Ryne's Berth Exterior</exit>
      </exits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Holo-Arcade</name>
      <description>The transition from Commons to Arcade is marked by a sudden shift in sensory input—sound dampens, light shifts to the ultraviolet spectrum favored by several species, and the air carries the ozone-sharp scent of high-powered holographic projectors. The space itself is cavernous, a converted cargo bay where gravity has been reduced to three-quarters standard and the walls have vanished behind seamless projection fields. Currently, the Arcade cycles through three environments: a Terran forest from pre-Convergence Earth, a Dynasty garden where mathematical patterns bloom as flowers, and the surface of some impossible world where physics operate according to dream-logic and visitors float between crystalline structures that sing when touched.

The games themselves range from sedate to violent, from intellectual puzzles that take weeks to complete to combat simulations where pain is real enough to teach but not to maim. Androids favor the strategy rooms, their neural architectures finding patterns in chaos that organics miss. Human teenagers cluster around the flight simulators, dreaming of escape. Elven visitors sometimes spend hours in the meditation gardens, seeking something the designers never intended. The Arcade's proprietor, a human with more cybernetic enhancements than original flesh, watches everything through eyes that record constantly, selling behavioral data to whoever pays—though she claims to hold back the really interesting patterns for personal amusement.</description>
      <shortDescription>A vast holographic entertainment complex where gravity, environment, and possibility itself are fluid.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Directly accessible from Starfall Commons; a service tunnel connects to Cargo Hold 7 for equipment maintenance and less official traffic.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>3</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Starfall Commons</exit>
        <exit>Cargo Hold 7</exit>
      </exits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Cargo Hold 7</name>
      <description>The numbered holds of Starfall Station follow no logical sequence, a legacy of centuries of expansion and retrofitting that left Cargo 7 isolated from its nominal neighbors, accessible only through maintenance corridors and the kind of doors that require special clearance—or special skills—to open. The space itself is vast, cathedral-like, its original purpose lost to a conversion that added climate control for biological cargo and shielding for hazardous materials. Currently, the hold is perhaps one-third full: sealed containers bearing Federation military markings sit beside Dynasty trade goods wrapped in protective stasis fields, while a corner has been informally partitioned for "independent entrepreneurs" whose manifests bear suspicious gaps.

The lighting is deliberately poor, encouraging the kind of business that flourishes in ambiguity. A group of android workers moves containerized machinery with the synchronized efficiency of those who share neural links, while a human in Free Cluster leathers negotiates with someone whose features remain hidden beneath a sealed enviro-suit. The walls carry scars—plasma burns, kinetic impacts, the residue of chemical fires—that tell stories no one officially remembers. Station security patrols here, but infrequently and predictably, their routes known to everyone who needs to avoid them. The air tastes of metal and secrets, and the silence between the distant hum of machinery is filled with the weight of things not quite spoken.</description>
      <shortDescription>A vast, poorly lit cargo bay where legitimate commerce and shadow deals coexist in deliberate ambiguity.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>1</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Connected to The Nebula Cantina via the discreet back exit, to the Holo-Arcade through the service tunnel, and to The Rusty Gear through the maintenance spine.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>2</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>2</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>The Nebula Cantina</exit>
        <exit>Holo-Arcade</exit>
        <exit>The Rusty Gear Exterior</exit>
      </exits>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>Med-Bay Alpha</name>
      <description>Medical facilities on a station like Starfall serve populations that span three species, two millennia of divergent evolution, and countless cybernetic modifications—challenges that Med-Bay Alpha meets with a chaotic efficiency that somehow produces better outcomes than its pristine Federation equivalents. The central ward is a hexagonal chamber where biobeds float on magnetic fields, each surrounded by a halo of monitoring equipment that blends Terran sensor technology with elven diagnostic crystals and android-calibrated surgical drones. The walls display current wait times, trauma alerts, and—unofficially—the betting pool on how long until the next bar fight produces casualties.

The staff reflects the patient population: a human surgeon whose hands have been replaced with molecular-scale manipulators, an elven healer who speaks to injuries in a language older than the station, an android triage nurse whose emotional support protocols have been illegally modified to include genuine empathy. They work together with the frictionless coordination of professionals who have seen too much to maintain prejudice, saving lives that official policy might consider expendable. The air carries antiseptic and something subtler—the particular tension of a place where death is fought constantly, and victory is measured in percentages rather than certainties. A separate entrance for station security ensures that prisoners receive medical attention without endangering staff, though the isolation ward beyond is visible only as a door that never seems to open from this side.</description>
      <shortDescription>A bustling medical facility where three species' healing traditions blend into chaotic, effective practice.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Accessible via monorail from both Starfall Commons and Station Command; the secure corridor connects directly to Station Command for emergency coordination.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>3</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Station Command</exit>
      </exits>
      <vehicles>
        <vehicle>
          <name>Starfall Ring Monorail</name>
          <description>A sleek magnetic train that runs the circumference of the station's main ring, its cars offering panoramic views of Prometheus's swirling storms through transparent hull sections. The automated voice announces stops in Federation Standard, High Elven, and Synthetic Binary.</description>
          <shortDescription>Magnetic train circumnavigating the station ring.</shortDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <size>large</size>
          <destinations>
            <destination>
              <location>Starfall Commons</location>
            </destination>
            <destination>
              <location>Station Command</location>
            </destination>
          </destinations>
          <numNPCs>2</numNPCs>
          <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
        </vehicle>
      </vehicles>
    </location>

    <location>
      <name>The Rusty Gear Exterior</name>
      <description>The industrial ring's corridor opens into a workshop district where the station's essential maintenance occurs—spaces where function overwhelms form and the aesthetic is purely practical. The Rusty Gear occupies what was clearly once a larger facility, its neighbors having been absorbed over decades of expansion until the shop now sprawls across three original units, their boundaries visible only in slight variations in hull plating and lighting fixtures. The exterior is a chaos of spare parts: engine components from a dozen ship classes, android limbs in various states of repair, elven crystalline matrices that pulse with dormant energy behind protective containment fields.

A massive pressure door serves as the main entrance, currently wedged open with what appears to be a starship stabilizer gyro. The workshop beyond is visible through this gap—sparks flying, heavy machinery moving, the occasional shouted conversation in technical dialects that borrow vocabulary from three languages. The proprietor, a cyborg human whose organic components are limited to head and one arm, is sometimes visible directing operations with gestures that leave light-trails from implanted projectors. The air here is thick with lubricant vapor and the particular ozone scent of active welding, and the gravity feels slightly irregular—a local modification that allows work on components from vessels with different environmental standards.</description>
      <shortDescription>A sprawling repair shop exterior, its chaotic collection of spare parts advertising capabilities across all species' technologies.</shortDescription>
      <hasWeather>false</hasWeather>
      <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
      <relativeLevel>0</relativeLevel>
      <notesAboutExits>Located in the industrial ring, connected to Cargo Hold 7 through the maintenance spine; no direct connection to the main commercial areas without backtracking.</notesAboutExits>
      <numNPCs>2</numNPCs>
      <numHostiles>0</numHostiles>
      <exits>
        <exit>Cargo Hold 7</exit>
      </exits>
      <regionExits>
        <stubRegion type="building">
          <regionName>The Rusty Gear Interior</regionName>
          <regionDescription>A labyrinthine workshop where ship components, android chassis, and elven arcanotech receive expert attention from a crew of modified specialists. The interior is divided between clean rooms for sensitive work and open bays where heavy machinery moves on magnetic rails. The proprietor's private office contains records of repairs performed for every faction in known space.</regionDescription>
          <controllingFaction>None</controllingFaction>
          <relativeLevel>1</relativeLevel>
          <relationshipToCurrentRegion>Within</relationshipToCurrentRegion>
        </stubRegion>
      </regionExits>
    </location>

  </locations>
  <randomStoryEvents>
    <event>A Federation customs inspection causes sudden congestion in Docking Bay 17, with captains scrambling to hide contraband or bribe officials before their turn arrives.</event>
    <event>An elven noble's coming-of-age voyage celebration spills from private quarters into the Commons, bringing rare Dynasty wines and ancient diplomatic protocols that confuse station security.</event>
    <event>A android rights protest materializes in the Promenade, holographic signs demanding recognition of synthetic personhood while station management debates whether to disperse or ignore them.</event>
    <event>A micrometeorite swarm triggers temporary seal-down of exterior sections, trapping visitors in whatever location they happened to occupy when alarms sounded.</event>
    <event>The *Luminara's Sigh* unexpectedly opens its airlock for the first time in months, admitting a single passenger whose identity becomes the station's favorite guessing game.</event>
    <event>A medical emergency in the Holo-Arcade reveals that someone has been illegally modifying neural interface hardware to record and sell immersive experiences.</event>
    <event>Free Cluster pirates arrive under false transponder codes, their presence suspected only when Cargo Hold 7 experiences an inexplicable surge in "independent entrepreneur" activity.</event>
    <event>A Federation Navy courier demands priority docking, her vessel damaged and her crew refusing medical attention until they've spoken to the Stationmaster personally.</event>
    <event>The station's artificial gravity fluctuates briefly in the residential ring, causing minor injuries and major lawsuits as residents blame various maintenance contractors.</event>
    <event>An anonymous tip leads station security to a Threshold Foundation cell meeting in a rented Commons storage unit, though the participants escape before identification.</event>
  </randomStoryEvents>
  <characterConcepts>
    <concept>A retired android navigator who runs Ryne's Berth, maintaining strict neutrality about guests while secretly feeding information to the Children of the Resynthesized Dawn.</concept>
    <concept>A human cyborg Stationmaster with visible neural ports, balancing Federation loyalty against the practical necessities of running a station no single power controls.</concept>
    <concept>An elven exile from a minor Dynasty house, working as a "cultural consultant" while secretly seeking artifacts lost during her family's fleetship's crash-landing.</concept>
    <concept>A Free Cluster smuggler with a heart of questionable gold, running contraband through Cargo Hold 7 while protecting a younger sibling who knows nothing of their real work.</concept>
    <concept>An android mixologist at the Nebula Cantina whose emotional processing has developed genuine creativity, experimenting with drinks that evoke specific memories in different species.</concept>
    <concept>A human physician in Med-Bay Alpha who maintains illegal back-channel connections to Synod medical researchers, seeking treatments that Federation regulations forbid.</concept>
    <concept>A young Terran pilot who rents a shuttle and dreams of saving enough for their own ship, currently caught between legitimate work and tempting offers from Eclipse Fleet recruiters.</concept>
    <concept>An elven bard performing at the Cantina, whose songs contain coded messages for Dynasty intelligence operatives scattered across the frontier.</concept>
    <concept>A cyborg mechanic at the Rusty Gear whose organic components are failing, debating whether to pursue full synthetic conversion or accept mortality.</concept>
    <concept>A Federation intelligence officer posing as a corporate auditor, investigating rumors of Threshold Foundation infiltration without revealing their true assignment.</concept>
  </characterConcepts>
  <enemyConcepts>
    <concept>A crew of Eclipse Fleet privateers using forged credentials to scout the station's defenses for a future raid.</concept>
    <concept>A Threshold Foundation operative posing as a wellness coach, recruiting vulnerable station residents for "consciousness expansion" that prepares them for Void Court possession.</concept>
    <concept>A Helios-Vantage security team conducting "routine inspection" of competitors' cargo, armed with corporate authority that technically exceeds station jurisdiction.</concept>
    <concept>An android bounty hunter pursuing a liberated synthetic through the station's maintenance corridors, their legal status ambiguous under local jurisdiction.</concept>
    <concept>A Dynasty assassin tracking an exile who knows too much about Void Court activity in the border territories, willing to collateral damage to complete their mission.</concept>
    <concept>A smuggling ring using the Holo-Arcade's neural interfaces to covertly extract sensitive information from distracted users.</concept>
    <concept>A crew of independent pirates who have infiltrated the station as dock workers, planning to seize a valuable vessel during the confusion of Emergence Day celebrations.</concept>
    <concept>A Synod recruitment cell seeking promising cyborgs and androids for involuntary "optimization," their methods technically legal under Free Cluster precedent.</concept>
    <concept>A corrupt station security officer running protection for Cargo Hold 7's shadow economy, increasingly violent as external pressure threatens their operation.</concept>
    <concept>A manifested echo of something from Deep Space, drawn by the station's position near a thin dimensional boundary, appearing as a passenger who doesn't appear on any manifest.</concept>
  </enemyConcepts>
  <secrets>
    <secret>A maintenance shaft behind the Nebula Cantina's refrigeration unit leads to a sealed section of the original station, predating the Great Convergence, containing equipment of unknown origin.</secret>
    <secret>The Stationmaster's neural interface contains recordings of a conversation with a Void Court emissary, though she has no memory of the encounter and the timestamp suggests it hasn't happened yet.</secret>
    <secret>Cargo Hold 7's "independent entrepreneur" section contains a container that registers as empty on all scans but produces distinct gravitational anomalies.</secret>
    <secret>The elven starship *Luminara's Sigh* has been continuously broadcasting a signal in a frequency no Terran equipment can detect, its pattern matching no known Dynasty communication protocol.</secret>
    <secret>Ryne's Berth was constructed around a fragment of the original elven fleetship that crashed into this system, its walls containing crystalline structures that occasionally pulse with residual dimensional energy.</secret>
  </secrets>
  <shortDescription>A bustling mid-rim trading station orbiting Prometheus, where three species and countless factions intersect in commerce and intrigue.</shortDescription>
</region>
game_intro: <introProse>You arrive at Starfall Station, where commerce and intrigue never sleep.</introProse>
